chapter_context.summary: Solomon prepares to build the Temple, forming an alliance with Hiram of Tyre for cedar and fir timbers; he establishes a labor force of quarry workers and lumberjacks.

outline.text: Alliance with Hiram of Tyre for Temple Materials
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Alliance with Hiram of Tyre for Temple Materials (5:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Solomon's Conscripted Labor and Quarrying Work
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Conscripted Labor and Quarrying Work (5:13-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 5, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
